name: Tourdera Trail: Montseny Loop
name_original: Río La Tordera y Can Cervera desde Montseny — circular por el Parque Natural del Montseny
description: Hike the moderate 4.8-mile Tourdera Trail: Montseny Loop, gaining 894 feet in 2 hours 21 minutes through the Montseny Natural Park.
region_intro: Hiking around Montseny offers diverse landscapes within a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, characterized by varied ecosystems including holm oak, beech, and fir forests. The region features prominent mountain peaks such as Turó de l'Home (1,706 m) and Les Agudes (1,703 m), providing challenging hikes and panoramic views. Abundant water features, including streams and waterfalls, contribute to lush valleys and scenic routes. This rugged terrain, carved by rivers and cliffs, supports a rich variety of flora and fauna, making it a notable destination for Montseny hiking. Best hiking trails around Montseny The most popular hiking route is Tourdera Trail: Montseny Loop, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular path through the Montseny landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Turó de l'Home — circular from Montseny, a difficult 10.9 miles (17.6 km) path. This challenging route ascends one of the highest peaks in the region, offering extensive views. Local hikers also love the Turó de l'Home Loop Trail, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Montseny is defined by diverse forests, prominent mountain peaks, and abundant water features. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to demanding long-distance paths. The routes in Montseny are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Montseny's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect in Montseny?
A: Montseny offers a diverse network of trails, ranging from easy walks to demanding long-distance paths. The region is characterized by varied ecosystems, including holm oak, beech, and fir forests, alongside prominent mountain peaks and abundant water features. You'll find options for all fitness levels, with 4 easy, 19 moderate, and 18 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Montseny?
A: Yes, Montseny has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails allow you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357821789/tourdera-trail-montseny-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourdera Trail: Montseny Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers a circular path through the landscape, suitable for many hikers.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Montseny?
A: For experienced trekkers, Montseny offers challenging routes that ascend prominent peaks and traverse rugged terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358505775/turo-de-l-home-circular-from-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home — circular from Montseny&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.9-mile (17.6 km) path that climbs one of the highest peaks, providing extensive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357929882/turo-de-l-home-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.9-mile (14.3 km) route through mountainous terrain.

Q: Where can I find the best panoramic views in Montseny?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider hiking to the prominent peaks within Montseny. Ascending Turó de l'Home (1,706 m), the highest point, offers spectacular vistas of the region, and on clear days, you can even see the sea. Other peaks like Les Agudes (1,703 m) and Matagalls (1,697 m) also provide excellent viewpoints. You can also find great views from highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1560958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view from the Collformic road&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features to see while hiking?
A: Water is a defining characteristic of Montseny, with numerous streams, river pools, and spectacular waterfalls. The Tordera River basin and its tributaries create lush vegetation and scenic routes. The impressive Salt de Sallent waterfall is a notable highlight, often found along routes like the Ruta de les Aigües. The park also boasts hundreds of natural springs.

Q: Are there historical or cultural sites to explore along the trails?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Montseny is rich in historical and cultural sites. Hikers can discover archaeological remains, Romanesque churches such as Santa Maria in Tagamanent, Sant Martí, El Brull, Sant Marçal, and Aiguafreda de Dalt. Notable castles include Montclús Castle and the Gothic military fortress of Montsoriu Castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5071250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Segimon Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is another significant site.

Q: What unique natural monuments can be found in Montseny?
A: Montseny is home to unique natural monuments that add to its charm. A particularly iconic attraction is the thousand-year-old Chestnut tree of Baga d'en Cuc, known for its immense, hollow trunk. The park's diverse ecosystems, encompassing Mediterranean, Euro-Siberian, and Boreo-Alpine biomes, also make the entire area a natural wonder.

Q: Is Montseny suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Montseny offers many trails suitable for families. The extensive network includes numerous well-marked paths of varying lengths and difficulties, ensuring there are options for all ages and fitness levels. Look for shorter, less strenuous routes that allow children to enjoy the diverse forests and natural features safely.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Montseny?
A: Montseny is generally dog-friendly, with many trails welcoming well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially in protected areas or near wildlife habitats. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog and clean up after them to preserve the park's natural environment.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Montseny?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Montseny, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush forests to panoramic mountain views, and the extensive network of well-maintained trails. The region's unique biodiversity and the presence of abundant water features are also frequently highlighted as enjoyable aspects.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Montseny?
A: Yes, Montseny features several excellent circular hiking routes that allow you to start and end at the same point, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357913455/sant-marcal-and-font-bona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Marçal and Font Bona Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5-mile (13.7 km) circular trail that takes you through beautiful landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358505775/turo-de-l-home-circular-from-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home — circular from Montseny&lt;/a&gt; also offers a challenging circular option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montseny?
A: Montseny offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout the year, but the best time depends on your preferences. Spring (April-May) brings lush greenery and blooming flora, while autumn (September-October) is popular for its vibrant fall colors. Summer (June-August) can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but higher peaks offer cooler temperatures. Winter (November-March) can provide unique snowy landscapes, though some higher trails might require appropriate gear or be less accessible.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, charming towns like Viladrau, known for its high-quality waters, serve as excellent starting points for hikes and offer amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in and around these villages. Additionally, some mountain passes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Te Restaurant & Bar&lt;/a&gt;, might have facilities for refreshments.
tips: The Gorg de Can Llavina is a natural pool under the bridge that crosses the river Tordera. It is easily accessible to cool off on a hot day.

The Llavina mill is located next to the Tordera, to the east of the farmhouse. It is a building of medieval origin that has been rebuilt and expanded on several occasions, most recently in the 20th century.
The Llavina mill dates back to 1084. The lack of an heir meant that they lost the name at the end of the 18th century. He was a gentleman son of La Llavina, Gabriel Llavina who in the 15th century went to live at the Molí de la Llavina del Montseny; and over the years it changed the name of what until then was the Molí del Cup de l'Ollic. As usual, the mill was restored on several occasions, after it was damaged by a flood. Inside the mill there is an inscription that says "RESTAURADO POR RAFAEL CASTAÑE 1897".

Church of Romanesque origin, enlarged and transformed by the reforms of 1767 and 1872. The bell tower has a square tower, with four lumbar arches on each side, as decorative elements. The facade is a gable, modified in 1767. It has a round window that illuminates the nave. There are three chapels on each side and at the bottom of the nave, the polygonal presbytery.

faqs.question: Where does the Tourdera Trail: Montseny Loop start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for this specific loop isn't detailed, many sections of the broader Tourdera Trail in Montseny are accessible from villages within the Montseny Natural Park. Parking availability varies by location, but generally, you can find parking in or near these villages. It's always a good idea to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate, meaning it requires a reasonable level of fitness. While some sections of the wider Tourdera Trail are very easy and family-friendly, this particular route involves a notable elevation gain of around 272 meters over 7.7 kilometers. It's best suited for hikers with some experience, but active families might enjoy it if they are prepared for the ascent.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape, including deciduous forests, shady ravines, and lush riverside environments. Key highlights include the tranquil The Tordera Stream, the picturesque Gorg de Can Llavina, and the historic Molí de la Llavina. You'll also pass by the charming Sant Julià del Montseny Church, offering glimpses into the region's cultural heritage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Montseny region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are often considered ideal. In spring, the forests are vibrant green, and wildflowers bloom. Autumn brings stunning fall foliage. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, so an early start is recommended. Winters can be cold, and higher sections might see snow, though this specific loop is at a moderate elevation.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Tourdera Trail: Montseny Loop?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within the Montseny Natural Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Montseny Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for hiking the trails within the Montseny Natural Park, which is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and practicing Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring paths through deciduous forests, some shady ravines, and sections alongside the Tordera Stream. You'll encounter natural trails that can be uneven in places, including an Uphill section. Sturdy hiking shoes are recommended for comfort and stability.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find parts of the broader Tourdera trail, as well as segments of the Els Tres Monts and Sender dels Miradors. It also briefly crosses parts of the GR 5.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants nearby for after the hike?
faqs.answer: The Montseny region is dotted with charming villages that offer opportunities for cultural exploration and local cuisine. Depending on your exact starting and ending point, you can often find cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options in nearby towns to refuel after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate loop, essential items include plenty of water, snacks, sturdy hiking shoes, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A small first-aid kit, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a charged phone are also advisable. Given the varied terrain, trekking poles can be helpful, especially on the uphill and downhill sections.
